items.headline: Schengen 90/180 stays unchanged through 2027.
items.lede: The clock you keep in the ledger is still correct.
items.body: The European Council confirmed last week that the rolling 180-day window will remain the basis for short-stay calculation. Reciprocity arrangements with K-ETA holders are also unchanged.
items.quote: There will be no amendment in this term.

items.headline: Lisbon raises short-let tax from 8% to 11%.
items.lede: Rooms booked before May are grandfathered.
items.body: Câmara Municipal published the new rate in the Diário da República. Owners with active contracts before April 30 keep the previous rate until end of 2026.

items.headline: TAP cuts ICN–LIS direct, one-stop only after June.
items.lede: Plan extra eight hours into the next move.
items.body: Direct service to Incheon ends 1 June; routings via Frankfurt or Madrid become standard. The carrier cites aircraft rotation, not demand.

items.headline: Visa-free 90 days extended to all OECD passports.
items.lede: Border guidance is unchanged on departure tax.
items.body: The Ministry of Justice published Notice 2026-04 on April 24. Single-entry permission stays at 90 days. Multiple-entry remains case by case.

items.headline: Yodogawa fireworks scaled back, half hour shorter.
items.lede: The river will be quieter on August 9.
items.body: City officials cite heat protocols for the reduction. Viewing platforms north of Umeda will not open. Public transit remains on the festival schedule.

items.headline: Mokuren plum blossoms, one week late this year.
items.lede: Expo Park reports peak around April 10 next time.
items.body: Park records mark the latest first-bloom in twenty years. Cherry season is expected to follow by a similar margin. Shrines along the Yodogawa note no schedule change.

items.headline: 365-day visa-free renewed; reset by exit-and-return.
items.lede: The clock restarts only after one full day out of country.
items.body: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reaffirmed practice for non-Schengen passports. The Public Service Hall confirms the count is calendar-based, not rolling.

items.headline: Sulfur baths in Abanotubani close two weeks for repair.
items.lede: The smaller hammam at #5 stays open through Easter.
items.body: City Hall announced the Hill 2 closure in late April. Hot water lines are being replaced. The old bathhouse archive will travel to the Ethnographic Museum.

items.headline: Wine-grape harvest forecast steady at 250,000 tonnes.
items.lede: Kakheti expects a quieter season than last year.
items.body: The National Wine Agency posted preliminary figures for 2026. Saperavi and Rkatsiteli volumes both fall within the ten-year average. Export orders to South Korea are up 12%.
